title: Maa Simsa temple
tagline: Maa Simsa temple — spiritual town of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Maa Simsa Mandir or Simsa Mata Mandir is a Hindu temple located in Ladbharol region of the Joginder Nagar subdivision of Mandi district of Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Maa Simsa Mandir or Simsa Mata Mandir is a Hindu temple located in Ladbharol region of the Joginder Nagar subdivision of Mandi district of Himachal Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to Sharada Devi. While the main temple is located in the Simsa village, 30 km from the Baijnath town, 38 km from Joginder Nagar, and other one Simsa Mata temple in Daroh village, 17 km (10.56 miles) from Palampur.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sari Molag
topPlaces.description: Sari Molag is a village of Himachal Pradesh, under Tehshil Jaisinghpur, Kangra district, India.

topPlaces.name: Haler, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Haler is a village of Himachal Pradesh, under Tehsil Jaisinghpur, Kangra district, India. It is located between Dalu village and Thanpal village.

topPlaces.name: Jaisinghpur
topPlaces.description: Jaisinghpur is a small town and subdivision in Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, India. There are two tehsils: the main tehsil is in Jaisinghpur and the sub tehsil is in Alampur.

Haler is a village of Himachal Pradesh, under Tehsil Jaisinghpur, Kangra district, India. It is located between Dalu village and Thanpal village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Maa Simsa temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Maa Simsa temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Maa Simsa temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (136 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Maa Simsa temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Maa Simsa temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Maa Simsa temple?
faq.a: Stays in Maa Simsa temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Maa Simsa temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sari Molag, Haler, Himachal Pradesh, Jaisinghpur.
